name: Art Tatum
abstract: Arthur Tatum Jr. (, October 13, 1909 – November 5, 1956) was an American jazz pianist who is widely regarded as one of the greatest ever.

concerts.label: Stan Kenton and His Orchestra: Festival of Modern American Jazz
Midnight Performance: Stan Kenton and His Orchestra: Festival of Modern American Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I also played with Art Tatum for the first time during that period. He was working at a place called the St. Francis down on Eighth Street, and he came to town and he wanted some horns to play with him -- because he always did a single or a trio, you know, a duo... So he hired John Anderson and me a

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: He said, “Art Tatum.” Oh my goodness. I said, “What’d you say again?” Art Tatum and Benny Carter and myself. No bass. Just a trio. You got a record of that?
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: And Art Tatum — there’s never been anybody yet with his ability... And he had command of the harmonic and rhythmic ideas more than anybody I’ve ever known.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Wilton, Connecticut, November 21, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Keith Ingham
about_quotes.evidence: people like Benny Carter, Duke Ellington, Art Tatum, I mean Fats Waller... I think Art Tatum must have had one of the most brilliant minds in the field.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keith Ingham interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Flanigan
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t know it was just one of those college things you know, your buddy’s on TV playing with this old guitar player that used to play with Art Tatum.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Flanigan interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: I had no — I think it made it better as opposed to me going, “Hey, while you’re having that Ovaltine and trying to relax, tell me about Art Tatum.”
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 18, 2023 [interview 2 of 2]
